title: Modify placeholder text on Monday forms
excerpt: We are an international company managing software projects. We recently created some surveys using Monday.com forms. The goal is to gather feedback about our new Monday.com boards so we can continuously improve our proce…

posts.author: bkennedy_ebsco (Belinda Kennedy)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are an international company managing software projects. We recently created some surveys using Monday.com forms. The goal is to gather feedback about our new Monday.com boards so we can continuously improve our processes.

We were able to create multiple forms to translate the survey into multiple languages. However, there are some limitations. I was unable to translate placeholder text hard-coded by Monday. Please see the examples below.

placeholdertext789×697 46.1 KB

footer915×362 22.6 KB

I would like the ability to modify ANY text that displays to Monday.com form users from the form view settings.

Has anyone found a solution to this problem?

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
hey @bkennedy_ebsco

Nachliel from Easyapps here

Our monday.com app Easyform allows you to do just that, when the form is set to Hide labels & show placeholders mode you can edit the placeholder to whatever you want it to display.

See here for example:

Read about editing placeholders and our Easyform many other features in our comprehensive documentation site.

You can contact us at support@easyappshq.com, and we also provide free training sessions if you need one.

Nachliel
